identity.essence: Bridging corporate strategy, corporate affairs and stakeholder advisory through The One Thing — the single transformative action that cuts through complexity and creates clarity.
identity.positioning: For boards and executives navigating complex stakeholder environments, Duke Strategies is the strategic advisory firm that makes business strategies stakeholder-proof, by combining corporate strategy depth with communications craft, AI-driven stakeholder intelligence, and direct government and security expertise.

archetype.rationale: Strategic identity is Sage — knowledge, truth, analysis; the visual and surface realization is Ruler/luxury (see expression). meta.archetype=sage; website.archetype=ruler, website.aesthetic=luxury. The model preserves both: Sage governs who Duke Strategies is and how it thinks; Ruler/luxury governs how that identity is expressed visually.

personalityTraits: Authoritative — senior counsel tone, not corporate stiffness
Bold — challenger mindset; tells it like it is
Analytical — data and insights first, then judgment
Progressive — AI-forward, grounded in decades of experience
Decisive — The One Thing; cuts through complexity

company.name: Duke Strategies
company.description: Duke Strategies is a strategic communications and public affairs consultancy founded by former Hill+Knowlton Strategies executives. The firm combines deep strategic advisory expertise with AI-driven stakeholder intelligence to help organizations navigate complex political, corporate, and societal challenges across Europe and beyond.

services.name: Strategic Advisory
services.description: High-level strategic counsel for boards, executives, and leadership teams navigating complex corporate, political, and societal challenges. We develop stakeholder-proof strategies that align business objectives with external expectations.

services.name: Crisis Communications
services.description: Rapid-response crisis management and communications support. From preparation and simulation to real-time crisis management, we help organizations protect their reputation and maintain stakeholder confidence during high-pressure situations.

services.name: Stakeholder Mapping & Engagement
services.description: AI-driven stakeholder identification, mapping, and engagement strategy. We use proprietary analytics to map influence networks, assess sentiment, and design targeted engagement programs that build coalitions and neutralize opposition.

services.name: M&A Communications
services.description: Strategic communications counsel for mergers, acquisitions, and corporate transactions. We manage the narrative across all stakeholder groups — from regulatory bodies and media to employees and shareholders — ensuring smooth transactions and protected valuations.

services.name: Media Training & Positioning
services.description: Executive media training and public positioning programs. We prepare leaders for high-stakes media interactions, public appearances, and thought leadership campaigns through intensive coaching, message development, and simulation exercises.

services.name: Change Management Communications
services.description: Internal and external communications strategy for organizational transformation. We design and execute communication programs that build understanding, reduce resistance, and accelerate adoption during major change initiatives.

services.name: Issue Management
services.description: Proactive identification, monitoring, and management of emerging issues that could impact organizational reputation or operations. We combine media intelligence, political monitoring, and stakeholder analysis to anticipate and shape the narrative before issues escalate.

evidence.note: v1.1 June 2026 — primary source for archetype (Sage primary, Ruler secondary), personality traits, color roles, typography, imagery rules, motif spec, voice principles

website.archetype=ruler, website.aesthetic=luxury; layout (airy, symmetric, full-bleed hero, generous spacing, mega-footer), components (shadow cards, square buttons), colorApplication (darkSections, accent sparingly), motion (subtle fade, lift hover) — all Ruler/luxury realization
v1.1.0 2026-06-10 — confirms dark/coral split, motif token set (pillar width, gap, cap height, coral line colors), spacing scale, shadow depths, transition speeds
Positioning statement, core message, voice attributes (bold/direct/confident/experienced/no-nonsense), words to use vs. avoid — grounds identity.positioning and personality

Four brand pillars (Stakeholder-Proof Strategy, Intelligence-Led Advisory, Strategy Meets Communications, Crisis-Tested Government-Connected) — grounds Sage identity: knowledge-led, analytical, evidence-grounded

principles: Structure first: dark (#222222) carries all structural weight — headings, table headers, hero backgrounds, overlays. Coral is a brand signature, never a structural color.
Controlled restraint: coral (#FF7F66) appears sparingly as accent — CTAs, motif elements, accent lines, chart emphasis. Never large fills or full-image washes.
Architectural grounding: all imagery draws from Dutch bridge and infrastructure photography, black-and-white treated, with 50% dark overlay. Bridges are a literal expression of the brand promise.
Editorial authority: Montserrat SemiBold/Bold for all headings; generous letter-spacing on overlines; uppercase headings on web. Space Mono reserved for data and overlines only.
Geometric precision: the bridge pillar motif (vertical lines with horizontal beam connectors) is the sole recurring graphic element — structural, not decorative.

Ruler/luxury surface realization: airy spacing, symmetric layout, full-bleed heroes, mega-footer, shadow cards, square buttons, and controlled high contrast enact Ruler authority. The Sage strategic identity is what informs and fills that structure — not a competing signal.
signatureElements: Bridge pillar / geometric column motif in coral on dark, dark on light, white on coral
Black-and-white Dutch bridge photography with 50% dark (#222222) overlay
Coral (#FF7F66) accent line / bar used as section divider or card left-edge element
Montserrat SemiBold headings in near-black (#222222); uppercase on web with wide tracking
Space Mono for overlines and data labels (uppercase, tracking-widest)
White logo variant on all photography and dark backgrounds; coral/primary logo on light
antiPatterns: Coral as a large background fill, full-image wash, or heading color
Non-Montserrat typefaces in brand materials (including Arial/Helvetica substitutes)
Color photography in brand layouts (all photography must be desaturated/B&W before overlay)
Rotating, skewing, or adding effects to the bridge pillar motif
Placing the coral or grey logo on image overlays (white variant only on dark/photo surfaces)
Introducing off-palette colors without brand team approval
Hedging language, passive voice, or jargon — voice discipline mirrors visual restraint
requiredMotifs: Bridge / infrastructure photography (Dutch focus; geometric, moody, high-contrast)
Bridge pillar pattern as section divider, card accent, or background at low opacity
Coral accent bar (48px wide, 4px tall) as section header underline
